description: The Sessions tab's default project folder (~/conductor/workspaces/open-agents/provo, freshly selected on first load) fails to load its session list and surfaces the raw string 'Could not load sessions: [object Object]' instead of a real error message. No console error was emitted alongside it (checked via `agent-browser errors`), so there is no secondary signal for a developer either.
expected: A specific, non-technical error message (matching the app's own pattern elsewhere, e.g. the 503 NOT_CONFIGURED copy documented in STORY-050/031: 'Not watching a session — set WFLENS_SESSION_DIR.'), or at minimum the underlying error's .message stringified instead of the object itself.
actual: Literal text 'Could not load sessions: [object Object]' rendered inline where the session list should be; no console error to cross-check.
suggested_fix: Wherever this catch block builds the error text (likely `Could not load sessions: ${err}` or similar string-concat of a thrown non-Error object), use `err?.message ?? String(err)` or JSON.stringify the error payload so the real cause surfaces instead of the default Object.prototype.toString() output.
